mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 305 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, compressing equity earnings multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% monthly volatility or Rank IC > 0.15 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signal stagflation risks, causing equity market sell-offs through higher real rates and earnings uncertainty over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in stagflation scenarios exceed 1.2% or cross-asset correlation between CPI/FEDFUNDS and SPX drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures that reduce corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in tight-labor scenarios exceed 0.8% or UNRATE decline correlates negatively with SPX returns (Pearson < -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals expanding industrial output, improving corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in industrial growth periods fall below 2% or INDPRO growth correlates negatively with SPX (Pearson < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in HOUST and RSAFS indicates strong consumer and housing demand, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-demand scenarios fall below 1.5% or HOUST/RSAFS growth diverges from SPX returns (Z-score > 2).

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth above 150k signals robust employment, increasing consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-employment periods fall below 0.5% or PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with SPX (Pearson < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth acceleration above 2% signals economic strength, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDP growth periods fall below 3% or GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with SPX (Pearson < 0.25).
